governing_constraint: Support any IdM that can produce a verifiable JWT (JWKS + iss/aud). Ory Kratos/Hydra is the reference standard. flint-gate federates; it is NOT an IdP/authorization-server.

decisions.rationale: Admin router has zero authn today. Wire a tower middleware that verifies against a configured AuthProviderConfig (JWT or Kratos). Reuses tested primitives; no new crate, no new identity model; single-binary preserved. Satisfies 'Ory is standard' (Kratos session or Hydra JWT) AND 'any IdM with a JWT'.
decisions.fallback: dedicated admin bearer credential when no provider configured
decisions.integration_notes: New `admin_auth` config block referencing an AuthProviderConfig id. Loopback-dev keeps an explicit `none` opt-in so local workflows stay ergonomic.

choice: auth/jwt_mint.rs JwtMinter.mint (issue delegated token + act claim)
decisions.rationale: No Rust server crate abstracts RFC 8693 (Tier-1: AWS SDK, openai/codex, warp all hand-roll the x-www-form-urlencoded POST). Issuance + verification halves already exist. Gateway-local downscoping (verify subject_token via JWKS -> mint downscoped token with act claim) is the vendor-neutral guarantee for 'any IdM with a JWT'. Delegate-mode proxies to Ory Hydra where configured (Hydra owns 8693, with known external-aud caveats).
decisions.fallback: gateway-local-only (skip Hydra delegate mode in v1)
open_questions: delegate-mode (proxy to Hydra) in-scope now vs gateway-local-first (lean: local-first with a config seam)
Hydra external-token aud handling (ory/hydra#3723) if delegate mode is used

choice: hand-rolled RFC 7662 /introspect for gateway-minted tokens (reuse jwt_verify)
consume/proxy Hydra RFC 7662 for Hydra-issued tokens
hand-rolled grant_type=client_credentials (mint via JwtMinter, client store extends api-keys)
decisions.rationale: Hydra implements both RFC 7662 and client-credentials natively (first-class introspection endpoint). Duplicating them wholesale is off-identity; flint-gate adds only the local surface for its OWN minted tokens plus a federation seam that prefers Hydra where it is the AS.
decisions.fallback: JWT-bearer-only introspection first; opaque-token store deferred
rejected: standing up a parallel full OAuth2 authorization server
open_questions: opaque-token store: reuse api-keys table vs new store; bearer-JWT-only first?

choice: thread principal entity-type through authz/engine.rs::authorize (make_uid already generic)
NHI lifecycle store (issue/rotate/revoke) via Admin API + UI + audit
decisions.rationale: Cedar plumbing is close: make_uid(type_name, id) is already generic over entity type, so adding Agent::/Service:: principals alongside User:: is a targeted thread-through. Real work is the lifecycle store + revocation-takes-effect-on-next-authorize semantics. Keeps NHI a first-class policy principal without a new engine.
decisions.fallback: User principal + `kind` attribute (no new Cedar entity type) if distinct types prove heavy
rejected: external identity provider for NHI (off single-binary)
open_questions: distinct Cedar `Agent::` entity type vs `User` + kind attribute (lean: distinct type)
G2 delegate-mode (proxy to Hydra) in-scope now vs gateway-local-first
G4 agent principal: distinct Cedar Agent:: type vs User + kind attribute
G3 opaque-token store: reuse api-keys table vs new store; bearer-JWT-only first
Admin authn default posture: none-on-loopback opt-in vs always-require when admin_auth set
Carried: Redis-L2 as hard dep for accurate cross-replica budgets
